title: Subclass 500 Student visa - Home Affairs overview

sections.heading: What the Subclass 500 visa allows
sections.text: The official Home Affairs Subclass 500 page describes the Student visa as a visa that allows a person to stay in Australia to study full-time in a recognised education course. The page also states that applicants generally need to be enrolled in a course of study and meet relevant visa requirements.

sections.heading: Core application requirements
sections.text: The Home Affairs Student visa page indicates that applicants may need to provide evidence requested by the Department, including material relevant to English language ability, financial capacity, health insurance, and identity. The exact evidence may depend on the applicant's circumstances and requests made by the Department.

sections.heading: Document preparation for advice
sections.text: For legal or migration advice about a Subclass 500 matter, useful source documents include the visa refusal notice if one exists, the application details, any Department request for information, enrolment documents, and evidence relating to English, financial, and Genuine Student matters.
